content.problemStatement:
Phineas and Ferb have made the treehouse. They have now decided to spend time with Perry who is spending his off day (remember, Chef is dealing with Dr. Doofenshmirtz today). They have taught Perry about the binary number system. Now, to check whether Perry has understood properly, Phineas will ask some queries which Perry needs to answer correctly.

The tree contains NNN nodes and every node iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N) has a value AiA_iAi​ associated with it. All the edges are bidirectional. The tree is rooted at node 111. Phineas will ask QQQ queries in total. Each query can be of 2 types:



111 uuu vvv xxx - For every node rrr on the simple path between nodes uuu and vvv, apply bitwise OR operation on the associated value with the value xxx, i.e., replace ArA_rAr​ by Ar∨xA_r ∨ xAr​∨x, where ∨∨∨ denotes the bitwise OR operation.




222 kkk yyy - Calculate the maximum value of Ar⊕yA_r \oplus yAr​⊕y over all nodes rrr in the subtree of node kkk, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation. The associated values of all the nodes will remain unchanged after this operation.



Help Perry answer the queries. Afterall, Perry is just a platypus. He doesn't do much.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of testcases.
The first line of each test case contains 222 space separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers denoting AiA_iAi​.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain two space separated integers aaa and bbb, each denoting a bidirectional edge between the nodes aaa and bbb in the tree.
The next QQQ lines describe each query in the format given above.


Output Format
For each query of type 222, print the maximum possible XOR value in a new line.
constraints: 1≤a,b,u,v,k≤N1 \leq a, b, u, v, k \leq N1≤a,b,u,v,k≤N
Sum of NNN and QQQ over all test cases for a particular test file does not exceed 3×1043 \times 10^43×104 and 5×1055 \times 10^55×105 respectively.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The sample tree can be represented as follows:

For the first query, the nodes in the subtree of node 111 are nodes 111, 222, 333 and 444. Consider the bitwise XOR values with the corresponding node values.

7⊕1=67 \oplus 1 = 67⊕1=6
7⊕2=57 \oplus 2 = 57⊕2=5
7⊕2=57 \oplus 2 = 57⊕2=5
7⊕3=47 \oplus 3 = 47⊕3=4

The maximum of these values is 666. Therefore, the answer to this query is 666.
After the update operation, the tree looks like the following representation:

For the last query, the bitwise XOR values are:

7⊕5=27 \oplus 5 = 27⊕5=2
7⊕6=17 \oplus 6 = 17⊕6=1
7⊕6=17 \oplus 6 = 17⊕6=1
7⊕3=47 \oplus 3 = 47⊕3=4

The maximum of these values is 444. Therefore, the answer to this query is 444.
